questions.prompt: It's the middle of midterms, the room is getting cluttered, and everyone is stretched thin. What's your reaction?

options.label: I'd be stressed until things were neat and chores were back on track.
Some clutter is fine, but trash or food piling up would bother me.
My own area can get messy; I just need the shared space clear.
I can let chores wait until the busy week is over.
A messy room usually doesn't get to me until it's really bad.

questions.prompt: You have an 8 AM class tomorrow, but your roommate is studying late with their desk light on. How does that land with you?

options.label: I'd struggle to sleep unless the room was dark and quiet.
I can make it work with a sleep mask or earplugs if they keep it down.
It wouldn't bother me unless there was loud talking or music.
I'm usually up late too, so it probably wouldn't be a big deal.
It depends on what both of us have going on the next day.

questions.prompt: You're relaxing on a Saturday afternoon when your roommate brings two friends into the room without warning. What's your gut reaction?

options.label: I'd be pretty frustrated; I need a heads-up before guests come into our space.
I'd be a little annoyed, but I'd put on headphones or find somewhere quieter.
I'm okay with an occasional daytime visit, just not during study time or late at night.
I'd probably be happy to join them.
It depends on my mood and whether I need some space that day.

questions.prompt: You notice your roommate has been using your expensive laundry detergent without asking. What's that like for you?

options.label: I'd be annoyed and want to say something right away.
I'd rather wait and have a calm, direct conversation.
I'd feel better bringing it up casually so it doesn't become a big thing.
I'd feel awkward mentioning it and might just put it away instead.
Honestly, I wouldn't mind them using it.

questions.prompt: Your roommate asks if a friend from another college can sleep on your floor for the weekend. How do you feel about that?

options.label: I'd be very uncomfortable with an overnight guest in the room.
I'd be okay with it if it were rare and we talked about it well ahead of time.
A night or two is fine if I get some notice.
I'm pretty relaxed about overnight guests as long as I still have my bed.
I'd be into it if we could host them together.
